body:
## Purpose 

(For now) precipitation is treated as a grid mean variable. Therefore it should be moved out of edmf code and into ClimaAtmos parameterizations.

Closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/issues/1530
title: Move grid-mean precipitations out of EDMF and into parameterizations

body:
## Purpose 
This PR fixes a nasty bug in the [`vertical_transport_jac`](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/blob/7d1bba56137206cc16eeb1ee6cf4d86a617f3870/src/tendencies/implicit/wfact.jl#L26), for both the `first_order` and `third_order` upwinding cases.
This manifested only in some high-resolution cases (moist BW and moist HS) and only in a few columns of the Jacobian.

We were adding a reference epsilon, `ref_εw` to `ᶠw`, but for some of the columns, this resulted in cancellation errors.

This PR will close #1576 


## Content
Solution implemented: 
- [x]  Modified the `ref_εw` hack, but instead of using `eps(FT)`, we make sure to add `eps(ᶠw.u₃)` to avoid cancellation errors with `ᶠw.u₃`.

Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.


- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Fix Wfact upwind cases
